steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure being punctuated
steps.content: The phrase "which often held symbolic [BLANK] of birds, horses, and other animals" contains a verb "held" and its object. The blank requires a noun and punctuation to show how "symbolic significance" and "depictions of animals" relate to each other within this dependent clause.

steps.title: Apply the correct punctuation for related nouns
steps.content:
A semicolon separates two closely related noun phrases that express a single idea or relationship.

Here, "symbolic significance; depictions of birds, horses, and other animals" uses a semicolon to show that the significance is expressed through or consists of depictions—they are distinct but interconnected elements of what the patterns held.

The comma in choice D would flatten the relationship into a simple list; the colon in choice B would overstate the hierarchy; a period in choice A breaks the dependent clause.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
